executive_summary: Weekly sales ex-VAT closed at 340,453.64 with an overall margin of 17.71%, indicating significant pressure from COGS or waste relative to the theoretical scan margins available across departments.
Total waste of 8,560.50 represents 2.51% of ex-VAT sales, which is a material drag on profitability and requires urgent category-level review.

The fresh and perishable departments — Deli (1,250.61), Provisions (1,223.48), Fish (874.05), and Bread & Cakes (916.99) — account for the four largest individual waste streams and collectively represent over 4,264.13 of total waste.
Produce delivered sales of 33,731.20 and remains the highest-volume fresh department, but waste of 1,172.33 against a scan margin of 19.27% requires close monitoring to protect net contribution.

Provisions recorded a reported margin of 50.24% against a scan margin of 19.96%, a significant deviation that warrants investigation into stock valuation, closing stock accuracy, or purchases data integrity, as purchases figures are not fully populated in the P&L.

The consolidated department total shows sales of 68,404.14 with purchases recorded as 0.00, suggesting purchase data is incomplete or mapped incorrectly in the OPR feed this week — this must be resolved before the P&L is finalised.

Cigarettes is reporting a margin of 100.00% against a scan margin of 12.78%, which is clearly a data anomaly — purchases and COGS are null, and this distorts both the department and total P&L materially.

Several meat sub-departments (Lamb, Poultry) show negative COGS this week, driven by closing stock exceeding opening stock with no purchases data present, which further signals incomplete data capture across the fresh P&L.
biggest_wins: Deli achieved a reported margin of 98.45% on sales of 7,051.02, with COGS of only 109.64, reflecting strong sell-through relative to stock movement — scan margin of 42.98% suggests genuine strong performance even after adjusting for data gaps.

Produce delivered the highest sales volume among fully reported departments at 33,731.20, with COGS of 943.13 and a reported margin of 97.20%, indicating tight stock management and good sales velocity this period.

Provisions generated a gross profit of 10,725.53 on sales of 21,347.48, representing a 50.24% reported margin — even discounting potential stock valuation anomalies, the absolute cash contribution is the strongest of any individual department this week.

Bread and Cakes waste of 916.99 is high in absolute terms but sits within a category known for daily ordering cycles — if sales performance is strong, the contribution may still be positive and should be reviewed against sales data when available.

Meat department opening stock reduction from 45,315.00 to 31,412.04 across the consolidated fresh P&L suggests active stock reduction and reduced over-ordering, which is a positive operational indicator.
biggest_risks: Cigarettes margin is reported at 100.00% due to null purchases and COGS data — this is a critical data integrity issue that will misstate the overall store P&L and must be corrected with actual purchase figures before any management decisions are made.

Fish waste of 874.05 is disproportionately high and, set against a scan margin of only 15.80%, significantly erodes net contribution from the department — ordering volumes and display life protocols must be reviewed immediately.

Deli waste of 1,250.61 is the single largest waste line in the store this week. Against sales of 7,051.02, waste represents approximately 17.74% of Deli sales, which is unsustainable and indicates over-production or poor sell-through on prepared lines.

Provisions waste of 1,223.48 against sales of 21,347.48 represents approximately 5.73% waste on sales within that department, compounding the risk already present from incomplete purchases data in the P&L.

Lamb and Poultry both show negative COGS (-96.26 and -133.59 respectively) due to closing stock exceeding opening stock with no purchases recorded — this is a stock or data entry error that must be investigated and corrected.

The overall purchases figure for the consolidated department P&L is recorded as 0.00, which renders the COGS, gross profit, and margin figures for the total block unreliable — a full data reconciliation with the buying system is required this week.

Overall store margin of 17.71% is materially below the theoretical scan margins available in key volume departments (Deli 42.98%, Produce 19.27%, Provisions 19.96%), suggesting shrinkage, waste, and data gaps are collectively suppressing the reported P&L by a significant margin.

Grocery Impulse waste of 652.04 and Bread and Cakes waste of 916.99 together add 1,569.03 to the waste total — both categories should have tighter date rotation and promotional clearance plans in place to reduce end-of-line losses.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Margin reported as 100.00% due to missing purchases and COGS data — figure is not valid and requires immediate correction using actual purchase invoices before P&L sign-off.

Beef: Negative stock movement from 11,543.00 to 9,941.46 gives COGS of 1,601.54 with no purchases recorded — scan margin of 19.85% is reasonable but net result cannot be confirmed without purchases data. Waste of 282.12 noted.

Lamb: Negative COGS of -96.26 indicates closing stock (1,402.26) has increased above opening stock (1,306.00) with no purchases recorded — result is not credible and stock count accuracy should be verified. Waste of 108.81 noted.
Pork: COGS of 589.51 derived from stock movement alone with no purchases — scan margin of 22.20% is acceptable if purchases are in line. Waste of 166.33 is moderate but should be monitored.

Bacon and Loose SRP: COGS of 99.65 from stock draw-down only, scan margin of 10.38% is the lowest across all meat sub-departments — margin vulnerability if any waste or shrinkage is unaccounted for. Waste of 127.66 noted.

Poultry: Negative COGS of -133.59 due to closing stock exceeding opening stock with no purchases — data integrity issue, likely a stock count or system mapping error. Waste of 190.20 must be addressed as it is the highest individual poultry waste line.

Fish: COGS of 167.39 from stock movement only, but waste of 874.05 is extremely high relative to the department size and a scan margin of only 15.80% — this department is likely loss-making on a fully loaded basis this week.

Produce: Sales of 33,731.20 with COGS of 943.13 and reported margin of 97.20% — the margin figure reflects stock movement only with no purchases captured. Scan margin of 19.27% is the realistic benchmark. Waste of 1,172.33 needs active management.

Deli: Sales of 7,051.02 with reported margin of 98.45% against a scan margin of 42.98% — purchases data absent, but scan margin suggests genuine strength. Waste of 1,250.61 is the largest single waste line in the store and requires immediate production planning review.

Provisions: Sales of 21,347.48 with gross profit of 10,725.53 at 50.24% reported margin versus a scan margin of 19.96% — the gap is significant and likely reflects closing stock anomaly (250.05 vs opening 10,872.00). Waste of 1,223.48 is also high and needs attention.
action_plan: Resolve purchases data gap immediately: Purchases are recorded as null or zero across all fresh and tobacco departments — escalate to the systems or buying team to ensure all purchase invoices are correctly mapped and re-run the P&L before final submission.

Investigate Cigarettes P&L entry: Margin of 100.00% with null COGS is invalid. Obtain actual purchase data for the tobacco category and restate the department P&L — this single error is materially distorting the store total.

Investigate Provisions closing stock: Closing stock of 250.05 versus opening stock of 10,872.00 with no purchases recorded produces an unrealistic COGS and margin. Conduct a physical stock count and reconcile against the system before finalising the week.

Deli waste reduction plan: Waste of 1,250.61 against sales of 7,051.02 equates to approximately 17.74% waste on sales. Review daily production schedules, reduce batch sizes on slow-moving lines, and implement end-of-day markdown protocols to recover value before disposal.

Fish ordering review: Waste of 874.05 against a scan margin of only 15.80% makes Fish highly vulnerable to net losses. Reduce order quantities, review display life settings, and ensure daily stock rotation is being completed by department staff.

Bread and Cakes waste management: Waste of 916.99 this week — introduce a structured end-of-day clearance markdown process and review ordering frequency to align more closely with daily sales patterns, particularly mid-week where sell-through may be lower.

Provisions waste investigation: Waste of 1,223.48 must be broken down by product line — identify the top five SKUs driving waste and assess whether ordering, rotation, or display issues are the root cause. Assign a department owner to report back within 48 hours.

Lamb and Poultry stock count verification: Both departments show negative COGS this week due to closing stock exceeding opening with no purchases. Verify physical counts were completed correctly and that system entries reflect actual stock on hand at close of Saturday.

Meat department waste review: Combined meat waste of 756.79 (Beef 282.12, Lamb 108.81, Pork 166.33, Bacon 127.66, Poultry 190.20 less Fish reported separately) — review counter ordering against daily sales data and consider reducing prep quantities on slower-moving cuts.

Grocery Impulse waste action: Waste of 652.04 in Grocery Impulse — conduct a date check across all ambient short-life lines, move near-date stock to a clearance bay, and review ranging to identify any slow-moving SKUs that should be delisted.

Overall waste reduction target: Total waste of 8,560.50 at 2.51% of sales is above acceptable norms. Set a weekly waste reduction target of 10% (approximately 856.00 reduction) and assign department managers to submit individual waste logs by Wednesday each week for in-week review.

Data integrity sign-off process: Implement a pre-close data validation check each Saturday to confirm that purchases, opening stock, and closing stock are populated for all departments before the P&L is submitted — missing data this week has rendered multiple margin figures unverifiable.
